---
title: "Notes"
toc: false
---

Short conceptual write-ups for each tool covered in the course. Each
note is meant as a 10–15 minute read that complements the hands-on
[labs](../labs/index.qmd) and [assignments](../assignments/index.qmd).

## Planned notes

Notes will be added a few at a time as the course is built. The
sequence follows the module spine:

**Workflow opening (Week 1)**

- Week 1 — Why mathematicians need tooling

**LaTeX block (Weeks 2–6)**

- Week 2 — LaTeX, demystified
- Week 3 — Theorem-proof typesetting and aligned equations
- Week 4 — Figures, tables, and a bibliography that actually compiles
- Week 5 — Picking a paper to replicate

**R block (Weeks 7–10)**

- Week 7 — R as a calculator, then as a language
- Week 8 — `ggplot2` in one sitting
- Week 9 — Simulation and `set.seed`: why reproducibility matters

**AI module (Weeks 11–12)**

- Week 11 — A mental model for AI assistants and a verification habit
- Week 12 — Catching AI hallucinations: a checklist for math, code, and citations

**Workflow bridge and portfolio (Weeks 13–15)**

- Week 13 — When you'd reach for SageMath, SymPy, or a CAS
- Week 13 — A small portfolio repo, the easy way

For software install instructions, see
[resources/software-setup.qmd](../resources/software-setup.qmd) and
[labs/lab01-install-stack.qmd](../labs/lab01-install-stack.qmd).
